title: hymenophyllum sanguinolentum
intro: Hymenophyllum sanguinolentum, also known as the bloody fork fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Hymenophyllum sanguinolentum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
